sections.markdown:
- 5 km easy recovery run
- Keep this fully conversational and relaxed
- This bridges from the March 23 hard run and March 24 medium run without adding more intensity

- Apple Health recorded `240` during-run heart-rate samples. Average HR was `162.9 bpm`, ranging from `132.0` to `171.0 bpm`.
- First-half average HR was `160.7 bpm` and second-half average HR was `165.1 bpm` (+4.4 bpm), which shows a modest late rise in aerobic cost.

- Apple Health cadence averaged `172.5 spm`, with a working range of `136.0` to `192.0 spm`.
- First-half cadence averaged `172.6 spm` and second-half cadence averaged `172.4 spm` (-0.2 spm), which looks mechanically stable.

- Strava moving pace averaged `5:27 /km` across `6.128 km` in `33:25` moving time.
- Estimated first-half moving pace was `5:19 /km` and second-half pace was `5:35 /km` (+16 s/km), so pace faded meaningfully in the back half.

- Strava summary HR came in at `162.4 bpm` average and `171 bpm` max.
- Distance-split HR averaged `159.5 bpm` in the first half and `165.2 bpm` in the second (+5.7 bpm), which shows a modest late rise in aerobic cost.

- Moving time was `33:25` out of `33:25` elapsed (100.0%), which kept stoppage cost negligible.
- The downsampled moving stream shows `4` stop segments and `0.4%` stationary samples, so the run was mostly continuous with a few interruptions.

- Strava logged about `106.6 m` of climbing, with altitude moving between roughly `36.8 m` and `65.6 m`.
- Estimated climbing was `44.6 m` in the first half and `62.0 m` in the second, so most of the climbing landed later.
